records.description: CU Boulder lists ChatGPT Edu as the University of Colorado enterprise version of ChatGPT for teaching, research, and administrative productivity.
records.howToObtain: Complete the required AI training course, then use the ChatGPT Edu login portal with CU Boulder credentials.
records.costToUser: Base access is provided at no cost for eligible CU Boulder faculty, staff, and students.

evidence.sourceTitle: ChatGPT Edu - Office of Information Technology
evidence.evidenceSnippet: CU Boulder OIT says ChatGPT Edu is the University of Colorado enterprise version of ChatGPT and provides a secure environment for students, faculty, and staff; access requires required AI training and CU Boulder credentials.

records.description: CU Boulder identifies Microsoft Copilot as another approved AI tool alongside ChatGPT Edu.
records.howToObtain: Use the CU Boulder-approved Microsoft Copilot path; details are not stated on the ChatGPT Edu page.

evidence.sourceTitle: ChatGPT Edu - Office of Information Technology
evidence.evidenceSnippet: CU Boulder OIT says ChatGPT Edu is an additional AI resource and does not replace other approved AI tools such as Microsoft Copilot.

records.universityName: City St George's, University of London
records.description: City St George's Library Services says general-purpose GenAI tools such as ChatGPT are not recommended for carrying out research.
records.howToObtain: Not endorsed by Library Services for carrying out research.
records.costToUser: Third-party service; not subscribed by Library Services

evidence.sourceTitle: AI tools and techniques for study - City St George's Library guides
evidence.evidenceSnippet: City St George's Library Services says it does not subscribe to AI platforms, the listed AI research tools are not sanctioned or endorsed by the University, and it does not recommend using general-purpose GenAI tools such as ChatGPT for research.
